chips.text: medication plan documented if clinician decided
nerve conduction study report reviewed if available
foot care education documented if relevant
renal function reviewed if on neuropathic agents
dose titration plan documented if clinician decided
B12 or thyroid context reviewed if relevant
sleep quality reviewed if affected by symptoms
mood reviewed if affected by symptoms

speed_preset.text: nerve conduction study report reviewed if available
medication plan documented if clinician decided
foot care education documented if relevant

history_draft.text: Patient presents with follow-up for neuropathy. [Sensory symptoms]. [Pain/burning]. Distribution [distribution]. [Progression]. [Diabetes/B12/thyroid context]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Blood pressure documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Sensation (including monofilament) documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Reflexes (including ankle) documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Limb power documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Foot examination (ulcers/skin) documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered B12 reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Glucose/HbA1c reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Thyroid function reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Renal function reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Nerve conduction study report reviewed.

plan_options.text: Pain management plan documented if clinician decided
Foot care advice documented if discussed

clinical_blockers: The workflow identity covers generic neuropathy, while the primary source is neuropathic pain and both sources are adult-only.

Vibration or monofilament testing, foot inspection or care, HbA1c wording, nerve-conduction studies, blood pressure, generic blood tests, dose titration, fixed follow-up, and the structured history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, treatment choice, medicine dosing, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
